title: General Mills: Flawless Dividend But There's A Problem
text: There are times when dividend growth stops. Currently, the dividend yield of General Mills suggests overvaluation.

title: Consumer packaged food companies keep raising prices, ‘and Walmart is sick of it,' says analyst
text: Consumer-packaged food companies that have been steadily increasing prices to combat inflation may be facing a formidable opponent, according to a new report.

title: General Mills Recognized on the USA Today America's Climate Leaders 2023 List
text: MINNEAPOLIS--(BUSINESS WIRE)--General Mills (NYSE: GIS) has been recognized on the USA Today list of America's Climate Leaders 2023. This prestigious award is presented by USA Today and Statista Inc., the world-leading statistics portal and industry ranking provider. General Mills is committed to reducing greenhouse gas emissions by 30 percent by 2030 and achieving net zero emissions by 2050. The company's latest recognition was driven by its significant progress to reduce greenhouse gas emissi.

title: Kellogg: Higher Dividend And Upcoming Spin-Off Makes It More Attractive Than General Mills
text: Consumer staple stocks tend to have lower volatility in uncertain times because people continue to need food. Kellogg and General Mills are two consumer staple companies that are known for breakfast foods, albeit with more diversification.

title: ASBFY vs. GIS: Which Stock Is the Better Value Option?
text: Investors with an interest in Food - Miscellaneous stocks have likely encountered both Associated British Foods PLC (ASBFY) and General Mills (GIS). But which of these two companies is the best option for those looking for undervalued stocks?

title: 5 Solid Stocks to Buy Amid Continued Market Volatility
text: Stocks like Ollie's Bargain Outlet Holdings (OLLI), The Hershey Company (HSY),General Mills (GIS), Conagra Brands (CAG) and Lamb Weston Holdings (LW) assure risk-adjusted returns during market volatility.

title: Dig Into These 4 Food Stocks on Splendid Industry Trends
text: The Zacks Food-Miscellaneous industry players are benefiting from strong pricing actions and favorable demand amid cost inflation. These factors bode well for General Mills (GIS), Conagra Brands (CAG), Campbell Soup (CPB) and Lamb Weston (LW).

title: COTY Beats Earnings Estimates in Q3, Raises 2023 Guidance
text: COTY third-quarter fiscal 2023 results reflect improved earnings and sales on gains from Prestige & Consumer Beauty business.

title: The Retirees' Dividend Portfolio: John And Jane's April 2023 Taxable Account Update
text: The Taxable Account generated $1,760.46 of dividend income for April 2023 compared with $1,609.40 of dividend income for April 2022. The Taxable Account had a balance of $523.4K as of April 30, 2023, vs. $522.7K on April 30, 2022. The annualized cost basis yield is 5.38%.

title: General Mills Names Lanette Shaffer Werner Chief Innovation, Technology and Quality Officer
text: MINNEAPOLIS--(BUSINESS WIRE)--General Mills names Lanette Shaffer Werner Chief Innovation, Technology and Quality Officer. She succeeds Jodi Benson, who is retiring in July.

title: V&M Breakouts: Top Long-Term Growth And Dividend Stocks For May 2023

title: 3 Consumer Staples Stocks Investors Shouldn't Ignore
text: For those interested in a more conservative approach in the near-term with a Fed decision looming, these three low-beta stocks could be great considerations. Impressively, all are coming off a recent double-beat.

title: 7 Consumer Stocks to Watch as the Economy Rebounds
text: As the financial markets continue to grapple with concerns over financial stability, investors seeking solace and potential returns would want to consider consumer stocks to buy. These resilient enterprises focus on fulfilling essential needs and thus benefit from a powerful captive audience catalyst.
